mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate observations over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity, forcing earnings downgrades and driving negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 20-day returns in >60% of observations, or regime interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with stronger corporate revenue trajectories, producing positive short-term equity momentum as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows, or 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, borrowing costs severely constrain corporate leverage and consumer spending, leading to sustained negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample predictive accuracy over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals lingering supply chain bottlenecks, triggering immediate risk aversion and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines in recovery regimes produce positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances, or the regime-specific predictive power is indistinguishable from baseline.
